phase1.explanation:
Java NIO (New I/O) provides modern file handling:

Path interface:
• Represents file/directory paths
• Platform-independent
• More flexible than File class

Creating Paths:
• Paths.get("path") — creates Path
• Paths.get("parent", "child") — parts

Key Path methods:
• getFileName() — filename
• getParent() — parent path
• getNameCount() — number of parts
• resolve() — combines paths
• normalize() — removes . and ..

Files utility class (main attraction!):
• write(Path, byte[]) — writes bytes
• readAllBytes(Path) — reads all bytes
• readAllLines(Path) — reads lines as List<String>
• copy(Path, Path) — copies file
• move(Path, Path) — moves/renames
• delete(Path) — deletes file
• exists(Path) — checks existence
• createDirectories(Path) — creates directories

Options:
• StandardCopyOption.REPLACE_EXISTING — overwrite
• StandardCopyOption.ATOMIC_MOVE — atomic move

NIO vs File:
• NIO: cleaner, more features, better performance
• File: older, more familiar

Benefits of NIO:
• Works with symbolic links
• More efficient file operations
• Better error handling (throws exceptions)

phase2.errorMessage: Error: nonexistent.txt (The system cannot find the file specified)

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that uses NIO to:
1. Create a Path for "data.txt"
2. Write the following content to the file using Files.write():
   "Line 1: Hello"
   "Line 2: World"
   "Line 3: NIO is great!"
3. Read all lines using Files.readAllLines()
4. Print each line
5. Check if the file exists
6. Print the file size
7. Delete the file using Files.delete()
dialogueHints: Convert content to bytes for writing: "text".getBytes()
size() returns long (file size in bytes)
phase3.openingDialogue: NIO — the new way to handle files! 🚀
